seoTitle: Seddon vs Selby — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Seddon and Selby. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Seddon and Selby.
heading: Seddon vs Selby — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Seddon, Victoria and Selby, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Seddon and Selby have similar overall climate risk scores.

faq.question: Is Seddon or Selby safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Seddon has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Selby scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Seddon scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Selby scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Seddon or Selby?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Seddon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
